decomposition.slice_name: Render and manage placed built-in steps on the Builder canvas
decomposition.intent: A placed built-in step (today only the join built-in) renders as a distinct, position-ordered card on the Builder canvas, appears immediately after the join modal saves, and can be removed and edited — the full #209 capability delivered end to end through backend and frontend together.
decomposition.scope: Backend: extend get_pipeline in workflow/attach.py to emit built-in steps via get_builtin_steps, tag every step with a step_type discriminator ('function' | 'builtin'), and order the unified list by position; the /pipelines route passes the shape through unchanged. Frontend: in screen-builder.jsx, dispatch the canvas by step_type — render a distinct built-in StepCard variant (reusing the PaletteBuiltinCard accent + 'built-in' tag) with a config summary derived from builtin_config; key/remove/edit built-in steps by step_id via the built-in routes; and wire JoinModal for an edit branch that pre-fills from builtin_config and PATCHes on save. Immediate appearance after create and edit reuses the existing loadPipeline() re-fetch. Built-in CRUD endpoints (DELETE/PATCH /sources/{id}/attach-builtin/{step_id}) already shipped in #117; no new schema or migration.
acceptance: GET /pipelines/{source_id} returns each placed built-in step in steps[] with step_type='builtin', carrying its builtin_type and builtin_config, positioned by position among the function steps.
Function steps in the same response carry step_type='function' and still include their full nested functions[] payload (no regression).

A placed built-in step renders on the canvas as a card visually distinct from function-step cards (the 'built-in' tag + accent), showing a config summary (e.g. 'Join · <right source> · inner · N keys').
After the join modal saves a join step, the new built-in card appears on the canvas without a manual reload.
The built-in card's remove control calls DELETE /sources/{source_id}/attach-builtin/{step_id} and the card is removed from the canvas.
The built-in card's edit control opens the join modal pre-filled from the step's builtin_config; submitting in edit mode calls PATCH /sources/{source_id}/attach-builtin/{step_id}.
